description: A production-ready workflow management system to track the end-to-end e-commerce lifecycle from order capture through payment settlement and reconciliation. The primary workflow sheet logs each order’s sequential milestones with planned vs actual dates and calculated delays, enabling SLA monitoring, exception handling (fraud, stockouts, returns), and finance reconciliation.

sheets.name: Order-to-Payment Workflow Main

columns.name: Channel
columns.description: Sales channel (web, marketplace, mobile app, etc.)

columns.name: Order Created Date
columns.description: Date/time the order was created in the source channel

columns.name: SLA Policy ID
columns.description: Reference to SLA Master to derive planned dates per stage

columns.name: Order Owner User ID
columns.description: Current responsible owner for operational follow-ups

columns.name: Hold Reason Code
columns.description: Reason when order is on hold (lookup from Status & Reason Codes)

columns.name: Cancel Reason Code
columns.description: Reason when order is cancelled (lookup from Status & Reason Codes)

columns.name: Order Source
columns.description: Source system/order intake method (API, manual, import, marketplace feed)

columns.name: Validation Notes
columns.description: Notes on validation issues (missing address, pricing mismatch, etc.)

columns.name: Customer Email
columns.description: Customer email for notifications and verification

columns.name: Payment Method
columns.description: Payment method (Card, UPI, Wallet, BNPL, COD, etc.)

columns.name: Order Amount
columns.description: Gross order amount including tax and shipping (as per order)

columns.name: Fraud Score
columns.description: Fraud risk score from provider/rules engine

columns.name: Manual Review Required YN
columns.description: Whether order requires manual fraud review

columns.name: Warehouse ID
columns.description: Warehouse fulfilling the order (lookup to Warehouse Master)

columns.name: Substitution Allowed YN
columns.description: Whether substitution is allowed if item unavailable

columns.name: Package Count
columns.description: Number of packages created for the order

columns.name: Shipping Label Generated YN
columns.description: Whether shipping label has been generated

columns.name: Dispatch Confirmation YN
columns.description: Whether dispatch was confirmed (handover to carrier)

columns.name: Delivery Date Carrier
columns.description: Delivery date/time as per carrier feed

columns.name: Recipient Name
columns.description: Name of person who received the package

columns.name: Delivery Exception Type
columns.description: Delivery exception category (Address Issue, Customer Not Available, Damage, etc.)

columns.name: Net Invoice Amount
columns.description: Final invoice amount after discounts and tax

columns.name: Settlement Batch ID
columns.description: Gateway payout batch ID or COD remittance batch ID

columns.name: Fees GatewayCarrier
columns.description: Total fees deducted (gateway MDR, COD fee, etc.)

columns.name: Bank Statement Reference
columns.description: Bank statement line/UTR/reference used for reconciliation

columns.name: Variance Amount
columns.description: Difference between expected and reconciled amounts

columns.name: Resolution Owner User ID
columns.description: User responsible for resolving reconciliation variance

columns.name: Closure Notes
columns.description: Notes for closure and audit trail

columns.name: Last Updated By
columns.description: User ID who last updated the record

stages.stage_description: Order is created/imported from the channel and validated for completeness (address, items, pricing, taxes, terms).
Payment is authorized (for prepaid) or payment method is confirmed (COD/BNPL). Authorization reference captured.
Fraud checks performed; order is approved, flagged for review, or rejected.
Inventory is reserved/allocated against the order; backorder/substitution decisions recorded.
Warehouse executes picking and packing; package details captured.
Shipment is created and dispatched to the carrier; tracking numbers recorded.
Delivery is confirmed by carrier/customer; exceptions like RTO/damage captured.
Invoice is generated/posted; taxes calculated and recorded for compliance and accounting.
Payment is captured/collected and settlement initiated/confirmed (gateway payout or COD remittance).
Settlement is reconciled with bank statement; discrepancies resolved; order financially closed.

sheets.description: Primary workflow sheet tracking the complete order-to-payment lifecycle with sequential stages, planned vs actual dates, and delay calculations.

sheets.name: Customer Master
sheets.description: Customer reference data for lookups and validation.

sheets.name: Product Master
sheets.description: SKU/product catalog reference data for item validation and reporting.

sheets.name: Warehouse Master
sheets.description: Warehouse/location reference data for fulfillment and allocation.

sheets.name: Payment Method Master
sheets.description: Reference list of payment methods and processors used in checkout and settlement.

sheets.name: Tax  Jurisdiction Master
sheets.description: Tax jurisdictions, rates, and mapping to regions/tax classes.

sheets.name: SLA Master
sheets.description: SLA policies to derive planned dates for each stage based on order priority, channel, region, or payment method.

sheets.name: Status  Reason Codes
sheets.description: Centralized dropdown values for operational statuses and reason codes (auth failures, inventory exceptions, dispatch exceptions, cancellation reasons, variance reasons, etc.).

sheets.name: Channel Master
sheets.description: Reference list of sales channels and identifiers used for integration/import.

sheets.name: Order Import Dump
sheets.description: Optional dump/import sheet for bulk order ingestion from external systems (raw landing before validation and mapping to the main workflow).
